heading: Configure OneLogin for LDAP Authentication
body:
The following procedure configures OneLogin for authentication
with Atlas:

- To learn more about setting up the Okta LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) interface,   see the
  [Okta documentation](https://help.okta.com/en/prod/Content/Topics/Directory/LDAP-interface-main.htm).

- Note your `<okta-instance-id>`. You must provide it
  in several places during the configuration process.

  The instance name is located in the URL you use to sign in to your
  Okta account:

  ```sh
  https://<okta-instance-id>.admin.okta.com
  ```

* [Create a new Okta user](https://help.okta.com/en-us/Content/Topics/users-groups-profiles/usgp-add-users.htm)
  to use as the Atlas bind user. The bind user is an Okta
  user that you use to query the account and to authenticate
  database users' credentials when they connect to an Atlas
  database.

  > **Important:** Don't use your own user account for the bind user.

* Use the following template to determine the full Distinguished Name
  (DN) of your bind user:

  ```sh
  uid=<bind-user-email>,dc=<okta-instance-id>,dc=okta,dc=com
  ```

  For example, if your `<bind-user-email>` is
  `bind@example.com` and your `<okta-instance-id>` is
  `mdb-example`, your bind user's DN is:

  ```sh
  uid=bind@example.com,ou=users,dc=mdb-example,dc=okta,dc=com
  ```

If they don't exist already, create users in Okta that you want to
grant database access to:

- Navigate to your Okta People page.

- Click Add Person.

- Enter the user's details. Use email addresses for usernames.

  > **Note:** Avoid entering email addresses with plus symbols (`+`). The
  > Atlas LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) integration may encounter issues with email
  > addresses containing plus symbols.

- Click Save.
